title: Iran Annual Budget Laws (Pahlavi era + early Islamic Republic), 1301-1363 AH solar (1922/23-1984/85) — mirrored via Lam ta Kam legal database
source_org: Majlis Shoraye Melli (National Consultative Assembly, Pahlavi era) / Revolutionary Council (Shoraye Enghelab, 1979-1980) / Majlis Shoraye Eslami (Islamic Consultative Assembly) — original legislature of enactment; text mirrored by Lam ta Kam (lamtakam.com), a private Iranian legal-reference aggregator that republishes the Majlis Research Center's own law database
source_url: https://rc.majlis.ir (primary/official archive; UNREACHABLE this session, see failures) — mirrored at https://lamtakam.com
download_url: see per-file download_url_original in files[] below

files.name: budget-law-1301-foreign-ministry.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون بودجه سال ایت‌ئیل 1301 وزارت امور خارجه
files.approved: 26 Jowza 1302 AH solar (approx. mid-1923 CE)
files.note: Foreign Ministry appropriation act for AH-solar year 1301 (~1922/23) — pre-Reza-Shah era; budgeting was done ministry-by-ministry, not as one consolidated national law, at this date; uses the old Turkic-derived 'ایت ئیل' year-naming convention still in administrative use at that time alongside the solar Hijri calendar.

files.name: budget-law-1301-justice-ministry.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون بودجه سال ایت‌ئیل 1301 تشکیلات قضایی و اداری وزارت عدلیه
files.approved: 1 Dey 1301 AH solar
files.note: Justice Ministry (Vezarat-e Adliyeh) judicial/administrative appropriation act for the same year, ministry-by-ministry budgeting era.

files.name: budget-law-1301-public-works-ministry.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون بودجه سال ایت‌ئیل 1301 وزارت فوائد عامه
files.approved: 1 Mizan 1301 AH solar
files.note: Vezarat-e Favayed-e Ammeh (Ministry of Public Benefits/Works, predecessor of later Roads/Public Works ministries) appropriation act.

files.name: budget-law-1341-whole-country.html
files.approved: 17 Aban 1343 AH solar (approved retroactively, ~2 years after the fiscal year; approx. Nov 1964 CE)
files.note: FIRST full consolidated 'whole country' (کل کشور) national budget law found in this session — single article (ماده واحده) with 66 tabsare (riders/notes). States total revenue 86,326,243,863 rials and total expenditure 90,440,043,863 rials for FY1341 (1962/63). Officially published in Official Gazette (روزنامه رسمی) issue 5839, Majlis term (دوره) 21.

files.name: budget-law-1360-whole-country.html
files.approved: 07 Mordad 1360 AH solar (~29 Jul 1981 CE)
files.note: First post-revolution budget passed by the newly elected Islamic Consultative Assembly (Majlis Shoraye Eslami) rather than the transitional Revolutionary Council. Total budget 3,165,981,700,000 rials (general revenue 3,037,000,000,000 + earmarked/vezarat-specific revenue 128,981,700,000). Opens with 'Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim.'

files.name: budget-law-1361-whole-country.html
files.approved: 26 Esfand 1360 AH solar (~17 Mar 1982 CE)
files.note: Total budget 3,104,626,793,000 rials. Iran-Iraq war ongoing; note the total is roughly flat vs FY1360 despite wartime pressures.

files.name: budget-law-1362-whole-country.html
files.approved: 26 Esfand 1361 AH solar (~17 Mar 1983 CE)
files.note: Total budget 5,816,156,938,000 rials — nearly double FY1361's total, reflecting wartime spending growth and inflation.

files.name: budget-law-1363-whole-country.html
files.approved: 30 Esfand 1362 AH solar (~21 Mar 1984 CE)
files.note: Total budget 6,550,834,894,000 rials, of which the 'general government' (بودجه عمومی دولت) portion is 4,087,830,110,000 rials — continued rapid nominal growth.

files.name: budget-supplement-law-1357-whole-country.html
files.law_title_fa: لایحه قانونی متمم بودجه سال 1357 کل کشور
files.approved: 16 Esfand 1357 AH solar (7 Mar 1979 CE)
files.note: SUPPLEMENTARY (متمم) budget bill for the LAST full Pahlavi fiscal year (1357 = 1978/79), enacted just weeks after the revolution (11 Feb 1979) by the Shoraye Enghelab (Revolutionary Council) acting as interim legislature since Majlis had been dissolved. Authorizes up to 145 billion rials in additional spending, mostly public-sector wage/pension adjustments. This is a LAYEHE QANUNI (legislative decree with force of law), not a Majlis-passed QANUN, reflecting the legislative vacuum of the transition. The original FY1357 main budget law (passed by the last Pahlavi Majlis before its dissolution) was NOT located in this session — only this post-revolution supplement was found.

files.name: budget-supplement-law-1358-whole-country.html
files.approved: 5 Dey 1358 AH solar (~26 Dec 1979 CE)
files.note: SUPPLEMENTARY budget bill for FY1358 (1979/80), the FIRST full fiscal year of the Islamic Republic, again enacted by the Revolutionary Council (Majlis Shoraye Eslami's first election was not until spring 1980). Authorizes 285 billion additional rials, mostly utility-price compensation and a Supreme Labour Council wage increase. The main FY1358 original budget law/decree was NOT located in this session — only this supplement.

files.name: budget-law-1343-revised-whole-country.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون بودجه اصلاحی سال 1343 کل کشور
files.approved: 4 Mordad 1343 AH solar (~26 Jul 1964 CE); signed 8 Mordad 1343; published 31 Mordad 1343; Official Gazette 5682
files.note: REVISED (اصلاحی) budget law for FY1343 -- amends the original FY1343 appropriation after the fact. Revenue 140,952,677,000 rials / expenditure 144,444,162,000 rials. Status: منسوخ (repealed/superseded, as expected for a decades-old annual law).

files.name: budget-law-1344-whole-country.html
files.approved: 27 Esfand 1343 AH solar (~18 Mar 1965 CE); signed 1 Farvardin 1344; published 30 Farvardin 1344; Official Gazette 5871
files.note: Main consolidated FY1344 national budget law. Revenue 175,046,000,000 rials / expenditure 176,662,000,000 rials (per the law's own written-out figures; the digit-string totals for the general+earmarked revenue components sum to 58,301,631,000 + further categories).

files.name: budget-law-1346-whole-country.html
files.approved: 28 Esfand 1345 AH solar (~19 Mar 1967 CE); signed 29 Esfand 1345; published 17 Farvardin 1346; Official Gazette 6444
files.note: Main consolidated FY1346 national budget law. Revenue and expenditure both 217,231,910,000 rials (balanced on paper).

files.name: budget-law-1352-whole-country.html
files.approved: 27 Esfand 1351 AH solar (~18 Mar 1973 CE); signed 29 Esfand 1351; published 2 Ordibehesht 1352; Official Gazette 8225
files.note: Main consolidated FY1352 national budget law -- the fiscal year just before the 1973 oil-price shock quadrupled Iran's oil revenue, useful as a 'before' baseline. Revenue and expenditure both 692,731,602,000 rials.

files.name: budget-law-1353-revised-and-1354-whole-country.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون بودجه اصلاحی سال 1353 و بودجه سال 1354 کل کشور
files.approved: 15 Aban 1353 AH solar (~6 Nov 1974 CE); signed 19 Aban 1353; published 14 Azar 1353; Official Gazette 8787
files.note: COMBINED law: (a) amends article B of the FY1352/FY1353-revised budget law's single article, resetting FY1353 revenue+expenditure to 2,082,635,827,000 rials each -- more than TRIPLE FY1352's 692.7bn rial budget, direct evidence of the 1973-74 oil-shock windfall hitting the government's books; (b) also sets the original FY1354 budget within the same single article. Two fiscal years' worth of primary-source figures in one document.

files.name: budget-law-1355-whole-country.html
files.approved: 21 Esfand 1354 AH solar (~12 Mar 1976 CE); signed 27 Esfand 1354; published 15 Farvardin 1355; Official Gazette 9102
files.note: Main consolidated FY1355 national budget law. Title uses '2535' -- the short-lived Imperial ('Shahanshahi') calendar Mohammad Reza Shah introduced in 1355/1976 (counting from Cyrus the Great's coronation), alongside the ordinary AH-solar year 1355, before it was abandoned in 1357/1978. Revenue 2,960,248,448,000 rials / expenditure 3,105,248,000,000-ish rials (deficit-budgeted, unlike the balanced-on-paper earlier-1350s laws).

files.name: budget-amendment-note85-law-1356-whole-country.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون اصلاح تبصره 85 قانون بودجه سال 1356 کل کشور
files.approved: 1 Bahman 1364 AH solar (~21 Jan 1986 CE, i.e. passed nearly a decade after the fiscal year it amends); Official Gazette 11940
files.note: PARTIAL/derivative document only -- amends a single note (tabsare 85, on civil-servant pension/retirement calculations) of the FY1356 budget law; the ORIGINAL full FY1356 'kol keshvar' budget law itself was NOT located in this session despite multiple targeted searches (a genuine remaining gap). Kept because it is still a real primary legal text referencing and partially quoting FY1356 budget provisions, and its very late amendment date (1364) is itself a small data point about post-revolution legislative cleanup of Pahlavi-era budget law provisions.

files.name: budget-law-1358-whole-country.html
files.approved: 27 Mordad 1358 AH solar (~18 Aug 1979 CE); Official Gazette 52866
files.note: MAIN original FY1358 budget law -- the database previously only had the supplementary (متمم) FY1358 law (budget-supplement-law-1358-whole-country.html); this is the base enactment it amends. Total 'general government budget' (بودجه عمومی دولت) 2,463,000,000,000 rials, of which general revenue 2,358,042,000,000-ish rials. First full-year Islamic Republic budget passed by the Revolutionary Council-era apparatus, a few months before the more famous FY1360 law (first passed by an elected Majlis).

files.name: budget-bill-1358-whole-country.html
files.approved: Approved as law 25 Mordad 1358 AH solar; published 22 Shahrivar 1358; Official Gazette 10066 (note: despite the page title using 'لایحه' (bill/draft), the metadata shows this WAS enacted -- appears to be an earlier or parallel official registration/printing of essentially the same FY1358 general budget enactment as budget-law-1358-whole-country.html, same total 2,463,000,000,000 rials figure verbatim; kept as a distinct document since it carries a different Gazette registration number and slightly different tabsare count (38 vs the other's structure), suggesting it may be a companion volume (e.g. an earlier legislative-process draft that was gazetted) rather than a true duplicate.

files.name: budget-forex-law-1364-whole-country.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون بودجه ارزی سال 1364 کل کشور
files.approved: 2 Mehr 1364 AH solar (~24 Sep 1985 CE); signed 14 Mehr 1364; published 25 Mehr 1364; Official Gazette 11836
files.note: FOREIGN-EXCHANGE budget law for FY1364 -- a distinct, specialized companion law authorizing the government to allocate up to USD 15,000,000,000 in foreign-currency receipts per attached tables, separate from (and narrower than) the general rial-denominated national budget law. War-economy-era forex rationing/allocation document (Iran-Iraq war ongoing) -- valuable for FX-history purposes even though it is not the general budget law for FY1364 (which was NOT located in this session, a remaining gap).

files.name: budget-law-1365-whole-country.html
files.approved: 26 Esfand 1364 AH solar (~17 Mar 1986 CE); signed 28 Esfand 1364; published 11 Farvardin 1365; Official Gazette 11969
files.note: Main consolidated FY1365 national budget law. Revenue and expenditure both 7,465,265,286,000 rials -- Iran-Iraq war still ongoing, continued rapid nominal growth vs FY1363's 6.55tn rials.

files.name: budget-law-1368-whole-country.html
files.approved: 29 Esfand 1367 AH solar (~20 Mar 1989 CE); signed 1 Farvardin 1368; published 21 Farvardin 1368; Official Gazette 12849
files.note: Main consolidated FY1368 national budget law -- the FIRST full post-Iran-Iraq-War budget (ceasefire was Aug 1988, mid FY1367) and the year Ayatollah Khomeini died (Jun 1989) / first year of the post-Khomeini reconstruction era under new President Rafsanjani. Revenue and expenditure both 9,741,705,403,000 rials. Largest single file in this collection (291KB) -- extensive line-item detail.

files.name: budget-supplement-law-1369-whole-country.html
files.law_title_fa: قانون متمم بودجه سال 1369 کل کشور
files.approved: 27 Azar 1369 AH solar (~18 Dec 1990 CE); signed 1 Dey 1369; published 17 Dey 1369; Official Gazette 13358
files.note: SUPPLEMENTARY budget law for FY1369 -- adds 435,630,000,000 rials to the original FY1369 appropriation. The ORIGINAL FY1369 main budget law and FY1366/FY1367 (both years) were NOT located in this session despite targeted searches -- genuine remaining gaps in the 1364-1370 bridge.

files.name: budget-law-1370-whole-country.html
files.approved: 11 Bahman 1369 AH solar (~31 Jan 1991 CE); signed 14 Bahman 1369; published 6 Esfand 1369; Official Gazette 13394
files.note: Main consolidated FY1370 national budget law. Revenue and expenditure both 20,097,297,101,000 rials. CRITICAL BRIDGE DOCUMENT: FY1370 is the year immediately preceding data/raw/iran-plan-budget-org/annual-budget-laws/1371.pdf, the first year of that PRE-EXISTING collection -- this file closes the gap, giving the database an effectively continuous Majlis budget-law record from FY1360 through FY1401 (1981-2023) except for the still-missing FY1366, FY1367, FY1369(main) trio.

time_coverage: 1301, 1341, 1343-1346 (scattered), 1352-1358 (scattered, incl. combined 1353+1354), 1356 (partial/amendment only), 1357-1358 (supplement + full), 1360-1365, 1368-1370 AH solar (~1922/23, 1962/63, 1964/65-1991/92 CE) -- substantially denser than the original session's coverage but still NOT fully continuous; see notes and failures for exact remaining gaps (1302-1340, 1342, 1347-1351, 1357-main, 1359, 1366-1367, 1369-main).
frequency: annual (each file is one year's enactment)
units: Iranian rial (nominal, as stated in each law's own text); no inflation adjustment applied
license_terms: Public legal/legislative text (Iranian government law), inherently not copyrightable as a government enactment; mirrored via a private legal-database aggregator (Lam ta Kam) whose own site terms are unclear — text itself is the primary legal record and is treated as public domain government material, consistent with how iran-plan-budget-org/annual-budget-laws (1371-1401) was already handled in this project.
notes:
GOAL: find Iran's actual historical annual budget LAW texts (a constitutionally required annual enactment) further back than the existing data/raw/iran-plan-budget-org/annual-budget-laws/ collection, which only covers 1371-1401 AH solar (1992/93-2022/23), all sourced via Iran Data Portal (Syracuse). A prior agent's note in SOURCES.md (line 195) explicitly found NO Pahlavi-era budget documents on Iran Data Portal.

PRIMARY SOURCE ATTEMPTED FIRST: rc.majlis.ir (Majlis Research Center), which per web search DOES index budget laws going back to at least 1301 AH solar (e.g. https://rc.majlis.ir/fa/law/show/95619 for FY1341, https://rc.majlis.ir/fa/law/show/90200 for FY1360). However, rc.majlis.ir's origin server returned HTTP 502 Bad Gateway consistently in this session (both via curl and via the browser preview tool, confirmed twice with a delay in between) -- 'Website server is temporarily inaccessible', server code 7800. This matches a prior agent's SOURCES.md note that the site was 'migrating.' curl requests to rc.majlis.ir individual law pages return HTTP 200 but with only a client-side anti-bot JS challenge shell (ArvanCloud CDN 'arcsjs' cookie-computation-then-reload challenge, ~6KB) rather than real content -- this is a JS-execution requirement (any real browser executes it automatically), not an interactive CAPTCHA, so accessing it via the browser tool is normal web access, not a bypass; the blocker in this session was the ORIGIN SERVER being down (502), not the JS challenge itself.

FALLBACK SOURCE USED: lamtakam.com ('Lam ta Kam' -- a private Iranian legal-reference/AI-services aggregator) which republishes the FULL TEXT of the Majlis Research Center's own law database, including metadata (تاریخ تصویب/امضا/انتشار = approval/signing/publication dates, شماره ثبت روزنامه رسمی = Official Gazette registration number, شماره دوره = Majlis term number, وضعیت = still-in-force/repealed status). Confirmed working via plain curl + browser User-Agent, HTTP 200, full legal text present in the static HTML (no JS execution needed to read the law text itself, unlike rc.majlis.ir). Cross-referenced figures within each downloaded law (e.g. FY1341 revenue/expenditure figures, FY1360-1363 nominal rial totals) look internally consistent and plausible against known Iran-Iraq-war-era inflation, giving reasonable confidence the mirror is faithful to the original Majlis text, though it has NOT been independently cross-checked against the rc.majlis.ir original (unreachable this session) or against a paper/gazette source.

METHOD FOR FINDING SPECIFIC YEARS: lamtakam.com's own site search (https://lamtakam.com/search?s=law&q=<query>&s_s=parliament&p=<page>) works via plain curl (returns server-rendered result snippets including a preview of the actual law text) and supports pagination via &p=N. General Google web search for exact phrases like 'lamtakam.com "قانون بودجه سال 1362" کل کشور' also reliably surfaced individual law page URLs. Each candidate page was fetched and its actual body text (not just meta/title tags, which are populated client-side and appear as generic breadcrumb text like ' - مصوبات مجلس شورا' in the static HTML) was checked to confirm it matches the claimed fiscal year and contains real numeric budget figures -- NOT a bot-challenge stub -- before being counted as a successful download. See files[] notes for the exact figures found in each.

COVERAGE ACHIEVED THIS SESSION: 1301 (3 ministry-level acts, pre-consolidated-budget era), 1341 (first full consolidated 'کل کشور' law found), 1357 and 1358 (Revolutionary Council-era SUPPLEMENTS only, main original laws not located), 1360-1363 (four consecutive full consolidated national budget laws). Combined with the pre-existing iran-plan-budget-org/annual-budget-laws collection (1371-1401), the database's Majlis budget-law holdings now span from 1301 through 1401 AH solar (~1922-2023 CE, over a century), though with substantial gaps remaining: 1302-1340, 1342-1356, 1359, and 1364-1370 are NOT yet covered. Filling those gaps is a natural follow-up task; lamtakam.com's search-plus-pagination method demonstrated in this session should generalize to it directly.

DISTINCT LAW TYPES ENCOUNTERED (documented for future disambiguation): (a) 'قانون بودجه سال X کل کشور' = the main annual national budget law (what this project wants most), (b) 'لایحه قانونی متمم بودجه سال X' = a supplementary/amending budget bill/decree for the same year (common in 1357-1358 transition when ordinary Majlis legislative process was suspended), (c) 'قانون تفریغ بودجه سال X' = the budget SETTLEMENT/closing law (Iran's ex-post actual-outturn reconciliation act, passed 1-5+ years after the fiscal year -- economically valuable as it reports ACTUALS rather than the original appropriation, and exists as a separate, distinctly citable law for most years, e.g. FY1345's settlement law was found dated 1351, six years after the fiscal year), (d) 'قانون بودجه سال X مجلس شورای اسلامی/ملی' = the PARLIAMENT'S OWN operating budget (a much smaller sub-law, not the national budget -- explicitly excluded from this collection), (e) ministry-specific budget acts (pattern seen for FY1301, before the single-consolidated-law format was adopted).

CONTINUATION PASS (2026-07-13, main-thread agent, no sub-agents spawned): Extended the collection using a KEY METHODOLOGICAL DISCOVERY -- lamtakam.com and rc.majlis.ir (currently down) share the EXACT SAME internal law-ID numbering scheme (confirmed by cross-checking multiple IDs, e.g. FY1341 is id 95619 on BOTH sites). This means Google/web search results that surface a dead rc.majlis.ir/fa/law/show/<id> URL (Google still has many indexed from before the outage, showing accurate titles/snippets even though the live page 502s) can be immediately converted to a working https://lamtakam.com/law/parliament/<id> URL. This is far more precise than lamtakam.com's own site-search (which does fuzzy/OR-style keyword matching and returns mostly irrelevant results for exact-year queries). Method used: WebSearch for 'rc.majlis.ir "قانون بودجه سال <YEAR> کل کشور"' (and variants without the exact-phrase quotes, or replacing کل کشور with مصوب/other qualifiers when the exact phrase didn't surface a hit), extract the numeric id from any rc.majlis.ir/fa/law/show/<id> result, then fetch https://lamtakam.com/law/parliament/<id> directly via curl and verify the <h1> title matches the target fiscal year before counting it as a real find (several false-positive ids were caught and discarded this way, e.g. ones that turned out to be FY1384 or FY1382 despite surfacing in a search for an earlier year). 14 new files landed this pass, spanning 11 additional fiscal years (1343 revised, 1344, 1346, 1352, 1353 revised+1354 combined, 1355, 1356 partial, 1358 main+bill, 1364 forex-only, 1365, 1368, 1369 supplement-only, 1370). This closes nearly the entire FY1364-1370 bridge to the pre-existing iran-plan-budget-org 1371-1401 collection (only FY1366, FY1367, and FY1369's MAIN law remain missing in that bridge) and adds meaningful Pahlavi golden-age (1343-1358) density, though real gaps remain within 1347-1351 and at 1342/1359/1357(main)/1366/1367/1369(main) -- see failures[] for exactly what was attempted and came up empty. Reza Shah/early-Pahlavi era (1302-1340) was explicitly NOT reached this pass due to time allocation across three separate mission targets (Majlis gaps, CBI/Bank Melli bulletins, TSE index) -- flagged as the natural next Majlis-focused continuation target, and the same rc.majlis.ir-id-crossover method should apply equally well there once tried.

failures.attempted: rc.majlis.ir (Majlis Research Center) -- the official primary source, e.g. https://rc.majlis.ir/fa/law/show/95619 (FY1341) and https://rc.majlis.ir/fa/law/show/90200 (FY1360), identified via web search as hosting the exact same laws
failures.result: Origin server down: HTTP 502 Bad Gateway ('Website server is temporarily inaccessible', Server Code 7800), confirmed via both curl and the browser preview tool on two separate attempts a few minutes apart. Consistent with a prior agent's SOURCES.md note (line 38) that the site was 'migrating.' Individual law pages return HTTP 200 to curl but only a client-side anti-bot JS-challenge shell (ArvanCloud 'arcsjs'), not the 502 error page directly -- the 502 appears only after the challenge JS actually tries to load the real page in a browser. Recommend retrying rc.majlis.ir directly in a future session; if restored, it is the more authoritative/citable source and should supersede this lamtakam.com mirror where both exist.

failures.attempted: qavanin.ir (another Iranian legal database, seen hosting recent-year budget laws e.g. https://qavanin.ir/Law/PrintText/184705 for FY1395) as a possible alternate/cross-check source
failures.result: HTTP 403 on the homepage via curl; not pursued further as a Pahlavi-era source since it appears to only index modern-era (post-1370s) laws in the search hits found.

failures.attempted: khatkesh.net (another Iranian legal database, seen in search results with an FY1356 budget settlement law)
failures.result: curl SSL certificate error ('no alternative certificate subject name matches target host name'), not pursued (would require -k/insecure flag, avoided).

failures.attempted: Locating the MAIN (non-supplementary) original budget laws for FY1357 and FY1358
failures.result: Only supplementary/amending bills (لایحه قانونی متمم) were located for these two transitional years via lamtakam.com's related-items links and site search; the original enactments were not found in the time available. Worth a targeted follow-up search.

failures.attempted: Filling the remaining gap years (1302-1340, 1342-1356, 1359, 1364-1370) systematically
failures.result: Not attempted exhaustively in this session due to time budget; lamtakam.com's ID numbering does not correlate simply with fiscal year (Pahlavi-era laws found in the 95000-96000 ID range, early-1360s laws in the 90000-90900 range despite being chronologically earlier in the site's apparent ingestion order), so each additional year requires its own targeted search rather than simple ID arithmetic.

failures.attempted: Systematic search for FY1302-1340 (Reza Shah + early Mohammad Reza Shah pre-1341 ministry-by-ministry and early consolidated budget laws), FY1342, FY1347-1351, FY1356 (full law, only a partial amendment was found), FY1357 (full law, only the post-revolution supplement was previously found), FY1359, FY1366, FY1367, and FY1369 (main law, only the supplement was found) -- via both lamtakam.com's own fuzzy search and WebSearch for 'rc.majlis.ir "قانون بودجه سال <year> کل کشور"' variants
failures.result: No confirmed matching page found for any of these specific years/documents in the time allocated this pass. Google's index of the (currently-down) rc.majlis.ir site is incomplete/uneven -- some years surface immediately (e.g. 1344, 1346, 1352 all found on the first or second targeted query) while others (e.g. 1347-1351, a contiguous 5-year block) returned zero rc.majlis.ir hits across multiple query phrasings. This likely reflects incomplete search-engine indexing of rc.majlis.ir's ~2 million law pages rather than these laws not existing (Iran's constitution has required an annual budget law continuously since long before 1341) -- a real continuation opportunity exists here via a different discovery method (e.g. systematically paging through lamtakam.com's own 'مصوبات مجلس شورا' category listing sorted by date rather than relying on search-engine indexing, or trying rc.majlis.ir's dedicated law-search endpoint 'rc.majlis.ir/fa/law/search?lu_approve_reference=lar2' once/if the origin server comes back up) -- not pursued further this pass due to time budget across the three-part mission.
